title: Suspicious PowerShell spawned outbound beacon
summary: Encoded PowerShell command launched from Office macro context, followed by repeated outbound TLS to a low-reputation host on a 60s interval.

title: Credential dumping via LSASS memory access
summary: Non-standard process opened a handle to lsass.exe with read access; consistent with credential theft tooling.

title: Mass file rename consistent with ransomware
summary: Over 4,000 files renamed with an unknown extension in under two minutes on a shared volume.

title: Multiple failed logons then success (possible brute force)
summary: 37 failed 4625 events for svc_backup across 4 minutes followed by a single 4624 success from an unusual source workstation.

title: Unusual OAuth token grant to third-party app
summary: A user consented to a newly-registered third-party application requesting mail.read and files.read scopes.

title: DNS tunneling pattern detected
summary: High-entropy subdomains queried at a steady rate to a single second-level domain — possible covert channel.

title: Data staging: large archive written to temp
summary: 7-Zip created a 2.1GB archive under C:\Users\Public, no prior baseline for this host.

title: Internal port scan from workstation
summary: Single host opened short-lived connections to 300+ internal addresses on common service ports.

title: New admin account created off-hours
summary: A new account was added to Domain Admins at 03:14 local time outside any change window.
